_meta.author: Claude (va-legislature-refine overnight autopilot)
_meta.note: VA Senate batch SD-16 through SD-20 (state tier). VanValkenburg (D, SD-16), Jordan (R, SD-17), Lucas (D, SD-18), New Craig (R, SD-19), DeSteph (R, SD-20). Evidence from VA LIS 2026 roll calls, VPAP, Ballotpedia, ReproRising 2025 scorecard, VCDL 2025 record, Family Foundation report card, ChoiceTracker, Cardinal News. KEY DEVIATIONS (verified): Jordan AND DeSteph (both R) voted YES on the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment HJ3 (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026) — same crossover bloc as Tara Durant. Craig (R) held the line (NAY HJ3). Lucas (D) is chief patron of ALL THREE 2026 amendments (abortion SJR1, marriage SJR3, felon-voting SJR2).

profile.religion: Presbyterian (per VA Senate member page / LegiStorm; not emphasized publicly)
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico County), sitting since Jan 2024; defeated incumbent Siobhan Dunnavant Nov 2023; former Delegate HD-72 (2018-2024). History/U.S. Government teacher at Glen Allen HS, National Board Certified; the only public-school teacher in the Senate. B.A. History (Univ. of Richmond), M.A. History (VCU); father of three.

schuyler-vanvalkenburg.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-16 (Henrico); sitting since Jan 2024; term runs through 2028. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive HARD record. Co-patron + YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247 2025 / HJ1 2026); ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'); ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice' (Planned Parenthood Action endorsed). YEA on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026). Vocal opponent of Education Savings Accounts / school choice. Lead sponsor of legislation returning VA to the ERIC multistate voter-data partnership; in the Democratic majority that advanced felon-voting restoration (HJ2). In the Senate Democratic bloc that passed the 2026 ICE-cooperation restrictions (21-19 party line) and the $15 minimum-wage law; championed the 2026 gun-reform package (assault-weapons ban, magazine limit, red-flag expansion). No conservative deviations found. RECORD CORRECTION: SB1031 (homeschool religious-exemption repeal) belongs to Sen. Pekarsky, NOT VanValkenburg — do not attribute it to him.

0.note: Co-patron + YEA on the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (SJ247/HJ1); ReproRising 2025 A+ 'Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: ChoiceTracker 'Pro-Choice': voted the full abortion-access slate — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Action / REPRO Rising — accepted abortion-industry endorsement.

0.note: Voted YEA on HJ3, the amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026).

1.note: YEA to codify an affirmative same-sex-marriage right in the Virginia Constitution.

2.note: Voted for the Reproductive/Gender-Affirming Health Care Act — supports gender-affirming policy.

4.note: Supporting legislator on the marriage-equality amendment; advances LGBTQ-affirming policy.

0.note: Vocal opponent of Education Savings Accounts / private-school choice; argues vouchers 'defund public schools.'

2.note: In the Senate Democratic majority that passed the $15 minimum-wage law and expanded social spending (party-line 20-19).

1.note: Lead sponsor of returning VA to the ERIC voter-data partnership and in the felon-voting-restoration majority (HJ2) — expansionist, opposite of photo-ID/citizenship-verification restriction.

2.note: In the Senate Democratic bloc (21-19 party line) that passed bills ending 287(g) agreements and restricting local cooperation with ICE — opposite of anti-sanctuary/federal-enforcement-cooperation position.

1.note: In the Democratic majority that advanced the 2026 gun-reform package — assault-weapons ban, 15-round magazine limit, red-flag expansion.

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024; former Delegate HD-64 (2018-2024). Small-business owner (wine shop/restaurant), first-responder trainer; Windsor Ruritan. Undergrad Regent University. Married (née Brewer).

emily-jordan.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-17, sitting since Jan 2024 (former Delegate HD-64). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Genuinely conservative HARD record (Family Foundation report card 95% pro-family, 18 of 19 votes) with ONE notable deviation. KEY DEVIATION (verified, LIS roll call): voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. On the same day she held the conservative line on the other two amendments: NO on HJ1 (abortion) and NO on HJ2 (felon voting), so the marriage vote is a deliberate, isolated break, not a moderate drift. Pro-life voting (NO HJ1); pro-parental-notification (NO on SB19/HB1499 narrowing the explicit-material notice law; YES SB817; YES SB778 obscene-material-to-minor penalty); YES HB131 (religious accommodations in higher ed); fiscal-conservative (NO on the $15 minimum wage SB1); election-integrity (NO on expanded Sunday absentee SB438, NO on felon restoration); pro-police (NO on SB352 restricting LEO, YES SB55/SB111 penalties, NO on SB60 parole expansion); anti-sanctuary (NO on SB783/HB1441 ICE-restriction bills); strongly pro-2A (NO on assault-weapons ban SB749, red-flag SB495, storage mandate SB348).

0.note: Voted NO on HJ1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment (party-line R NO bloc, 01/16/2026); Family Foundation 95% pro-family.
DEVIATION: voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: YES to establish an affirmative same-sex-marriage right in the Virginia Constitution.
Voted NO on SB19/HB1499 (which narrow the parental-notification law by excluding library books) and YES on SB817 (parental notice of course-registration) — supports parental notification.

2.note: Voted NO on bills narrowing the sexually-explicit-instructional-material notification requirement — favors keeping explicit-content review broad.

3.note: Voted YES on SB778, making display of obscene material to a minor unlawful (penalty).

1.note: Voted YES on HB131, requiring public higher-ed institutions to reasonably accommodate students' religious beliefs/observances — conscience exemption for students.

2.note: Voted NO on SB1/HB1 ($15 minimum wage) and NO on related wage mandates — fiscal-restraint posture.

3.note: Voted NO on SB438 (expanding in-person absentee voting to additional Sundays) and NO on ERIC membership (SB57/SB52) — opposes mass mail-in / early-voting expansion.

0.note: Voted NO on SB352 (restricting law-enforcement officers from wearing facial coverings) — backed police against a restriction.

1.note: Voted NO on SB60 (expanding Parole Board powers / juvenile parole) — opposed sentencing leniency.

2.note: Voted YES on SB55 (sex offenders barred near children, penalty) and SB778 (obscene material to a minor) — proportional punishment.
Voted NO on SB783/HB1441 (restricting local law-enforcement cooperation with ICE) — opposed sanctuary-style limits, pro federal-enforcement cooperation.

1.note: Voted NO on the assault-firearms/magazine ban (SB727/SB749), red-flag orders (SB495/HB901), and the storage mandate (SB348).

profile.religion: Baptist (Deaconess, New Mount Olivet Baptist Church); some structured sources list African Methodist Episcopal — denomination conflict noted
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-18 (Portsmouth/Chesapeake); in the General Assembly since 1992; Senate President pro tempore and Chair of Finance & Appropriations. First female shipfitter at Norfolk Naval Shipyard (1971); first woman and first African American elected Senate Pro Tem. B.S. and M.A., Norfolk State University.

l-louise-lucas.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-18; Senate President pro tempore; Chair, Finance & Appropriations; in the GA since 1992. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Uniformly progressive and unusually activist HARD record: CHIEF PATRON of all three 2026 constitutional amendments — reproductive freedom (SJR1), same-sex marriage (SJR3, repealing the Marshall-Newman ban), and felon-voting restoration (SJR2). ReproRising 2025 scorecard A+ ('Champion'). VCDL 2025 record 0% pro-gun; chief patron of the 2026 assault-weapons ban SB749; Giffords 'champion.' Chief patron of SB1 ($15 minimum wage) plus paid-sick-leave (SB199), paid family leave (SB2), child-care assistance (SB3). As Finance Chair, killed Youngkin's private-school voucher proposal ('not going to happen') and declared his sanctuary-funding-restriction budget item dead. Chief patron of SB438 (expanded Sunday early voting) and SB322 (National Popular Vote compact). No conservative deviations.

0.note: Chief patron of SJR1, the reproductive-freedom constitutional amendment; ReproRising 2025 A+ 'Champion' — does not affirm personhood from conception.

1.note: Lead author/face of the abortion-rights amendment — opposite of abortion-abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Advocates of Virginia — accepted abortion-industry endorsement.

0.note: Chief patron of SJR3, the constitutional amendment repealing Virginia's same-sex-marriage ban and establishing an affirmative right to marry for 'two adult persons.'

1.note: Lead author of codifying same-sex marriage in the Virginia Constitution.

4.note: Sponsored the marriage-equality amendment — advances LGBTQ-affirming policy.

0.note: As Finance Chair, killed the private-school voucher/ESA proposal: 'not going to take any money from public schools for vouchers for private schools.'

2.note: Chief patron of SB1 ($15 minimum wage) and of paid-leave / child-care spending laws (SB199, SB2, SB3) — expanded mandates and spending.

1.note: Chief patron of SJR2 (automatic felon-voting restoration) — expansionist, opposite of citizenship-verification ID restriction.

2.note: Chief patron of SB438 expanding in-person early voting to additional Sundays — opposite of single-day in-person voting.

3.note: Expanded early/absentee voting access rather than tightening mail-in voting.

2.note: As Finance Chair, declared Youngkin's proposal to withhold funds from sanctuary localities dead — pro-sanctuary posture.

1.note: Chief patron of SB749, the 2026 assault-firearms / large-capacity-magazine ban; VCDL 2025 record 0% pro-gun.

profile.religion: Christian (self-identified; specific denomination/congregation not publicly documented)
profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-19 (Chesapeake/Virginia Beach), sitting since Jan 2024 (won Nov 2023). Sworn law-enforcement officer, Chesapeake Sheriff's Office (1987-2002); chief of staff to Sen. John Cosgrove (2002-2023); Chesapeake School Board 12 years (Chair 2013-2016); Fraternal Order of Police member. Western Branch HS; UVA Sorensen Program.

christie-new-craig.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-19 (Chesapeake/Virginia Beach), sitting since Jan 2024; full 2024-2026 voting record (NOT a thin newcomer). [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Consistent conservative record with NO cross-party deviation: NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, 2025), ReproRising 2025 'F'/Extremist, ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice' (18 documented anti-abortion/contraception votes); NAY on the same-sex-marriage amendment HJ3 (held the line — was NOT in the 5-Republican crossover bloc) and NAY on SB101. Family Foundation report card 95% pro-family (HARD third-party backstop). Career LEO + FOP member, chief patron of a law-enforcement-impersonation penalty bill; chief patron of a bill tightening the in-person-absentee window to 15 days. iVoterGuide survey positions (SOFT): pro-school-choice incl. vouchers/homeschooling, anti-CRT/'dirty books,' tax-cutting, secure-border, strong 2A ('none of the gun restrictions needed'). Several dimensions left null where only general campaign sentiment (not a vote) exists. NOTE: she took office Jan 10, 2024 — not a 2025 special election as the batch query speculated.

0.note: Voted NAY on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, 2025); ReproRising 'F'/Extremist; ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice' with 18 documented anti-abortion votes.
Voted NAY on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage amendment — held the conservative line (not among the 5 Republican crossovers).

1.note: NAY on HJ3 and on SB101 (marriage-license approval for all consenting parties) — opposes codifying same-sex marriage.

2.note: Campaign core value opposing transgender participation in girls' sports — affirms biological sex in policy.

0.note: iVoterGuide: strongly agrees with school choice including vouchers, tax credits, charter schools, and homeschooling; 12-year Chesapeake School Board member.

2.note: Campaign 'parents' rights' platform opposing 'Woke Indoctrination' and 'Dirty Books in Schools.'
iVoterGuide: advocates cutting individual income taxes and lowering corporate rates — fiscal restraint.

3.note: Chief patron of a 2025 bill tightening in-person absentee voting to 15 days before an election — narrows the early-voting window.

0.note: Career law-enforcement officer (15 yrs, Chesapeake Sheriff's Office) and FOP member; chief patron of a law-enforcement-impersonation penalty bill — affirmative backing of law enforcement.

1.note: iVoterGuide: 'none' of the proposed gun restrictions are needed; 'the Second Amendment says our rights shall not be infringed.'

profile.background: VA State Senator, SD-20 (Virginia Beach/Norfolk + Eastern Shore); in the Senate since 2016; former Delegate (2014-2016) and Virginia Beach City Council (2006-2014). Navy veteran (Desert Storm; naval special-warfare intelligence officer); B.S. Finance, Univ. of Maryland; defense-tech executive.

bill-desteph.notes_set: VA State Senator, SD-20 (Virginia Beach/Eastern Shore); in the Senate since 2016. [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement, state tier] Conservative HARD record (Family Foundation 95% pro-family; ReproRising 'F'; ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice') with ONE notable deviation. KEY DEVIATION (verified, multiple outlets): voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of only ~5 Republicans crossing over — biblical_marriage q0/q1 FALSE. Otherwise: NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247); patroned school-choice ESA bills (SB1290/SB558) and a parental-consent-for-sexually-explicit-materials bill (SB787); backed stricter photo-ID requirements (killed by the Democratic P&E committee 8-7); YES on the 2018 sanctuary-city ban (HB1257); NO on the 2026 assault-weapons ban SB749. Religion not publicly documented (null). Economic/public-justice dimensions left null — only self-reported endorsements, no isolated HARD votes located.

0.note: Voted NO on the reproductive-freedom amendment (SJ247, 21-19, 01/21/2025); ReproRising 'F'; ChoiceTracker 'Anti-Choice.'
DEVIATION: voted YES on HJ3, the same-sex-marriage constitutional amendment (Senate 26-13, 01/16/2026), one of ~5 Republicans crossing over — does not affirm marriage as one man and one woman.

1.note: YES to repeal the constitutional same-sex-marriage ban and establish an affirmative right to marry.

0.note: Patroned Educational Savings Account bills (SB1290, SB558) letting parents of qualified students apply for school-choice funds.

1.note: Patroned SB787, requiring parental consent before checkout of sexually explicit school materials — parental consent on minors' materials.
Backed stricter photo-ID requirements (and a shorter voter-registration-closure period); the Democratic Privileges & Elections Committee killed the photo-ID bill 8-7.

2.note: Voted YES on HB1257 banning 'sanctuary cities' — barring localities from restricting enforcement of federal immigration law.

1.note: Voted NO on SB749, the 2026 assault-weapons / large-capacity-magazine ban (Senate 21-19, 02/09/2026).
